title: A Midsummers Nice Dream

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening moment of the episode
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase opening - perfectly integrated but routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: Here's a story that gives new meaning to the words 'puff piece.'
jokes.setup: News anchor introducing a story about Cheech and Chong
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'puff piece' - journalism term and marijuana reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - news terminology meets drug humor

jokes.text: Cheech and Chong are from Springfield? Lucy and Desi are getting a divorce?
jokes.setup: Learning Cheech and Chong are from Springfield
jokes.punchline: Homer's non-sequitur about Lucy and Desi shows his confusion and outdated references
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer confusion mixing timeframes and topics - good character moment

jokes.text: their 'joint' partnership
jokes.setup: Describing Cheech and Chong's collaboration
jokes.punchline: Joint as both business partnership and marijuana reference
jokes.explanation: Obvious but functional pun - quotes around 'joint' make it a bit heavy-handed

jokes.text: Why does everything have to have an 'ometer' on it? And what is rain, man? It's like we're in the apartment under God and his fat sister overflowed the tub.
jokes.setup: Young Chong doing weather on TV
jokes.punchline: Stoner philosophy about rain being God's upstairs neighbor's overflow
jokes.explanation: Creative stoner metaphor for rain that captures the character's voice well

jokes.text: the Clamphitheater, the $800 million boondoggle based on nothing more than a cute play on words
jokes.setup: Describing the new amphitheatre
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on the pun while using it, plus absurd cost
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about bad puns while making one - typical Simpsons meta-humor

jokes.text: Who the hell are Cheech and Chong?
jokes.setup: Family discussing comedy legends
jokes.punchline: Generational gap - kid doesn't know classic comedy duo
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor setup for the escalating exchange that follows

jokes.text: Bart, Cheech and Chong were the Beavis and Butthead of their day. Who are Beavis and Butthead? I've failed as a parent.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain Cheech and Chong through modern reference
jokes.punchline: Bart doesn't know that reference either, Homer realizes he's failed
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating joke about generational references and parental failure

jokes.text: I swore the day my son was born he would appreciate stoner comedy. To the media room!
jokes.setup: Homer's parental failure revelation
jokes.punchline: His priority as a father was stoner comedy appreciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - absurd parental goals delivered with determination

jokes.text: Open up. It's the police. Oh, man. I got to get rid of my grass. He's flushing his pot down the toilet-- unnecessarily!
jokes.setup: Classic Cheech and Chong bit about police raid
jokes.punchline: Homer's commentary that the toilet flushing is unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Homer missing the point of the bit while over-explaining it

jokes.text: Back then, we'd play these albums again and again till they were filled with skips and clicks and pops. Which was about three times.
jokes.setup: Nostalgic description of playing vinyl records repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Records only lasted three plays before getting damaged
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about vinyl record fragility with perfect timing

jokes.text: These are comedy albums? The Noisy Minority. The Upside-Down Bubblegum Collective. The Regional Lampoon. The Plymouth Grok Experiment. Fro And Flo. Allan Sherman's Helter Shmelter.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning if these are comedy albums
jokes.punchline: List of ridiculous fake album names that parody real comedy albums
jokes.explanation: Creative fake album names but rapid-fire delivery dilutes individual impact

jokes.text: What are you guys doing up there? Hide the dope! We don't have any dope. Then, what did I just smoke?
jokes.setup: Police raid scene with Wiggum and Lou
jokes.punchline: Wiggum admits he just smoked something but they have no drugs
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence - perfect character comedy

jokes.text: Lou, I want you to confiscate these brownies. There's no drugs in these brownies. I said 'confiscate'!
jokes.setup: Lou explaining brownies are drug-free
jokes.punchline: Wiggum just wants to eat them regardless
jokes.explanation: Wiggum using police authority for personal gain - good character consistency

jokes.text: Uh, also confiscate me a T-shirt, uh triple-XL. I thought you said double-XL was a real wake-up call. That was for pants.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking for XL shirt
jokes.punchline: Reveals his pants size is bigger than his shirt size
jokes.explanation: Body humor with good character detail about Wiggum's weight issues

jokes.text: When they asked me to introduce Cheech and Chong, I said, 'How much?' And they said, 'We don't pay for introductions,' so I said, 'Then I don't need this crummy gig.' And they said, 'Yes, you do,' and I said... 'Ladies and gentlemen, Cheech and Chong!'
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining his negotiation backstory
jokes.punchline: Despite his protest, he immediately caves and does the introduction
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty - pretends to have standards but immediately compromises

jokes.text: I thought they'd do bits, but their most famous bit? Dave's not here! Say the line, man. They already know the line. What's the surprise? What's the point? The point is they still give us a shoebox full of 20s to do this.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about why they're doing the famous bit
jokes.punchline: Cheech cynically explains it's just for money
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on nostalgia acts and audience expectations

jokes.text: Dave's not gonna sell out anymore, man. I-I can't say it. What are you doing, man?! What am I doing? What am I... I'm experimenting, man. I'm growing as an artist, okay?
jokes.setup: Chong refusing to do the classic bit
jokes.punchline: Artist pretentions about 'experimenting' with comedy
jokes.explanation: Good satire of aging performers trying to stay relevant artistically

jokes.text: I'm a 72-year-old man. I've eaten eggs every which way they cook 'em. I can't play it safe anymore.
jokes.setup: Chong explaining his artistic rebellion
jokes.punchline: Using eggs as metaphor for life experience and risk-taking
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific metaphor that works for the character's stoner philosophy

jokes.text: Well, how about this? Chong's not here, man.
jokes.setup: Chong trying to modernize the classic bit
jokes.punchline: Replacing Dave with his own name in the catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Simple variation on the classic bit - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I can't do reefer comedy. I'm drunk. Two different animals.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer help with the comedy
jokes.punchline: Homer's taxonomy of intoxication-based comedy
jokes.explanation: Homer's logical but flawed reasoning about substance-based performance styles

jokes.text: Just because it's too hip for you, Smithers...
jokes.setup: Burns watching Cheech and Chong
jokes.punchline: Burns pretending to understand counterculture humor
jokes.explanation: Burns being out of touch while pretending to be hip - good character moment

jokes.text: I don't find this funny at all. My time being wasted was not wasted.
jokes.setup: Smithers responding to Burns about the comedy
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical statement about wasted time not being wasted
jokes.explanation: Smithers' dry wit with a logical contradiction that somehow makes sense

jokes.text: Will I get to meet Dave? There is no Dave. How about Don Johnson? It would be easier to meet Dave.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about joining as new Chong
jokes.punchline: Escalating impossibility of meeting fictional vs. real but inaccessible people
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about celebrity accessibility vs. fictional characters

jokes.text: And remember, on the road, the only vice you can indulge in is gluttony. Save lust and rage for me and the kids.
jokes.setup: Marge giving Homer guidelines for the road
jokes.punchline: Treating the seven deadly sins like a resource allocation problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Marge line treating sins as manageable household resources

jokes.text: Sweetie, don't worry. I'm gonna come back so horny and angry.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Marge's vice allocation
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically promising to save up negative emotions
jokes.explanation: Homer's innocent enthusiasm about terrible things makes it funny

jokes.text: The van made of weed. It's not made of weed. Oh. What's it made of? Hash? 'Shrooms? Lysergic acid diethylamide? No. The van is just made of van.
jokes.setup: Homer assuming everything is drug-related
jokes.punchline: Cheech's deadpan explanation that the van is made of normal van materials
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer's drug knowledge vs. Cheech's reality check

jokes.text: Can we get high from the fumes? No!
jokes.setup: Cheech blacking out Chong's face on merchandise
jokes.punchline: Homer still thinking everything can get him high
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Homer's drug obsession but getting repetitive

jokes.text: Snap, snap. Our friendship died with the first snap, and you buried it with the second.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying out with suspenders
jokes.punchline: Chong's dramatic reaction to suspender snapping
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with poetic language about suspender snapping

jokes.text: You ever notice how your nose makes boogers? What's up with that?
jokes.setup: Comedy audition attempt
jokes.punchline: Terrible observational comedy about boogers
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad comedy that parodies hack observational humor

jokes.text: I can't pay the rent. You must pay the rent. I can't pay the rent. You must pay the rent. I'll pay the rent. My hero. Aw, who am I kidding? I don't got the money.
jokes.setup: Classic melodrama bit with hero saving the day
jokes.punchline: Subverts the hero moment by admitting he's also broke
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of classic bit but execution is weak

jokes.text: I am your substitute teacher, Sister Mary Elephant. Class, attention, attention, class, class. Shut up. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Skinner doing classic Cheech and Chong bit
jokes.punchline: Perfect execution of the Sister Mary Elephant routine
jokes.explanation: Skinner's teaching background makes this callback work well for his character

jokes.text: Really? But Sister Mary's no-nonsense attitude is what inspired me to become an educator.
jokes.setup: Chong rejecting old material
jokes.punchline: Skinner's sincere connection to comedy bit as career inspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - taking comedy too seriously and personally

jokes.text: I want you to reinvent the wheel. No, de-invent the wheel. No. 'Re-unvent the whool.'
jokes.setup: Chong trying to give artistic direction
jokes.punchline: Increasingly nonsensical variations on 'reinvent the wheel'
jokes.explanation: Good stoner logic escalation with made-up words

jokes.text: A lot of people have cell phones, but I like to eat healthy, So I invented... the celery phone. Hello? Hello?
jokes.setup: Frink's innovation pitch
jokes.punchline: Celery phone is just actual celery, doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink invention that's literally what it sounds like

jokes.text: Wow. You are the un-funniest man I've ever seen. Which means, teaming up with you would be totally pushing the envelope.
jokes.setup: Frink bombing completely
jokes.punchline: Chong choosing him specifically because he's unfunny
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - turning complete failure into qualification

jokes.text: Mother, how did you get this number? It's a prop phone. Yes, I have your celery. I took the limpest stalk there was.
jokes.setup: Frink's celery phone bit continues
jokes.punchline: Pretending the vegetable phone actually connected to his mother
jokes.explanation: Extended bit commitment but pushing the joke too far

jokes.text: Is it really okay to just drop them into the pit where the Blockbuster used to be? Got a better idea?
jokes.setup: Returning overdue movies to defunct video store
jokes.punchline: Using the empty lot as a makeshift return box
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about changing retail landscape

jokes.text: Easter grass, Cable Ace Awards, aquariums full of broken pinball game parts, popped but uneaten Jiffy Pop still in the silver bubble, sample-sized shampoos, detergents and whiskeys
jokes.setup: Cataloging Cat Lady's hoarded items
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd collection of junk
jokes.explanation: Good detailed hoarding imagery but rapid-fire delivery reduces individual impact

jokes.text: I hate to say this about the Cat Lady, but I think she's crazy. She's a hoarder.
jokes.setup: Seeing the Cat Lady's massive hoard
jokes.punchline: Understatement about someone living in extreme hoarding conditions
jokes.explanation: Lisa's diplomatic understatement about obvious mental health issues

jokes.text: Like a reality show without the cameras. You mean, just reality. Yeah, that's what you think. Cut, print, mail to NBC.
jokes.setup: Marge comparing helping to reality TV
jokes.punchline: Bart revealing they're actually filming for TV
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about reality TV that subverts the family's good intentions

jokes.text: That's funny, Homer. Now we really have to work on the show. Oh, yeah, I get you-- 'work on the show.' Maybe we should start at 4:20, right? Marijuana reference! Yeah, I got it.
jokes.setup: Cheech mentioning work on the show
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstanding and over-explaining his own drug reference
jokes.explanation: Homer trying too hard to be cool and explaining his own jokes

jokes.text: Hard work? I thought this was gonna be fun. It is fun! The fun is the knowledge that our audience gets a good value for their hard-earned dollar!
jokes.setup: Homer disappointed about work
jokes.punchline: Cheech's completely joyless definition of fun
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's expectations and Cheech's workmanlike attitude

jokes.text: ♪ Cheech is really mad ♪ ♪ I wish that I had gone with Cho-ong. ♪
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to sing despite Cheech's anger
jokes.punchline: Homer regretting his choice and elongating Chong's name melodically
jokes.explanation: Homer's continued singing despite consequences is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Memory, All alone in the moonlight, I can smile at the old days, I was beautiful then... Ah, so beautiful!
jokes.setup: Cat Lady singing while family cleans
jokes.punchline: Delusional nostalgia about her past beauty
jokes.explanation: Standard delusional character moment but well-executed

jokes.text: A perfectly good hot lather dispenser. I can't count the number of times Homie's cried in anguish over his lukewarm lather.
jokes.setup: Marge finding random item in trash
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific justification for keeping junk based on Homer's habits
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures how hoarders justify keeping useless items with specific scenarios

jokes.text: All of J.D. Salinger's books, except Catcher In The Rye!
jokes.setup: Marge excited about books
jokes.punchline: Missing the one famous Salinger book everyone knows
jokes.explanation: Good literary joke about incomplete collections missing the key item

jokes.text: She's crazy, but I'd do her.
jokes.setup: Willie watching Marge become a hoarder
jokes.punchline: Willie's inappropriate sexual comment about mentally ill behavior
jokes.explanation: Typical Willie crudeness but feels cheap and potentially offensive

jokes.text: Hey, Sister? Hey, Sister? Class! Class! I got to go to the can, man. Shut up!
jokes.setup: Skinner continuing Sister Mary Elephant routine
jokes.punchline: Mixing the authoritarian teacher bit with stoner language
jokes.explanation: Good character integration of the callback but getting repetitive

jokes.text: That basketball was like a basketball to me
jokes.setup: Basketball Jones song about loving basketball
jokes.punchline: Tautological statement that basketball was like basketball
jokes.explanation: Circular logic joke in the song lyrics - mildly amusing absurdist moment

jokes.text: Oh! Why aren't you exactly like your stage persona?
jokes.setup: Cheech being intellectual at the museum
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated question about performer vs. person
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on audience expectations of celebrities

jokes.text: When do we have wacky adventures? Ah, that's just the movies, man. Can we at least get some French fries? Too high in trans fats. Stop speaking Spanish.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting typical comedy movie experiences
jokes.punchline: Cheech being health-conscious, Homer thinking museum talk is Spanish
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's disappointment and cultural ignorance

jokes.text: Skinner, his dessert menu is funnier than you. Yeah. Yes, but I'm funnier than the cocktail napkins. They're blank, Seymour. Yes, but there are two 'K' sounds in cocktail.
jokes.setup: Skinner bombing at comedy
jokes.punchline: Desperately grasping at phonetic humor in random words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner desperation - overthinking simple words for comedy

jokes.text: Yes, but there are two 'K' sounds in cocktail. Yes, there are.
jokes.setup: Skinner defending his comedy credentials
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to find humor in phonetics, confirmed by Chalmers
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Skinner's desperate academic approach to failed humor

jokes.text: Who has disturbed my tomb? Grampa, you're not dead. Well, wake me when I am.
jokes.setup: Grampa acting like he's in a tomb
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude about death and sleeping
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa death humor delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: It's disrespectful to the high standards people have come to expect from Cheech and Chunk.
jokes.setup: Cheech being serious about comedy standards
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces his partner's name as 'Chunk'
jokes.explanation: Simple name mistake but shows Cheech's psychological distancing from Chong

jokes.text: That's right. Now let's take it from the part where we sniff each other's butts. Yes, sir. Come on, sniff! That's a movie sniff. This is live theater. Now inhale, damn it.
jokes.setup: Cheech directing Homer in rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Increasingly demanding direction for degrading comedy bit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of artistic pretension applied to lowbrow comedy

jokes.text: That's a movie sniff. This is live theater. Now inhale, damn it.
jokes.setup: Cheech directing Homer in butt-sniffing routine
jokes.punchline: Cheech's intense artistic direction about proper sniffing technique
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity of theatrical direction for crude comedy bit

jokes.text: I can't believe the crazy house would throw out all these forks.
jokes.setup: Marge now hoarding from the Cat Lady's discarded items
jokes.punchline: Calling the mental institution 'crazy house' while exhibiting same behavior
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Marge becoming what she just helped cure

jokes.text: Do the Yankees hoard pennants? Does Marrakesh hoard intrigue?
jokes.setup: Marge defending her collecting
jokes.punchline: Pretentious analogies that don't make logical sense
jokes.explanation: Excellent hoarder rationalization with absurd but poetic comparisons

jokes.text: Styrofoam shaped like old computers!
jokes.setup: Marge finding more treasures
jokes.punchline: Completely useless item that mimics obsolete technology
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist item but part of rapid-fire sequence that dilutes impact

jokes.text: If Art Garfunkel could forgive that untalented twerp, Paul Simon you can do this.
jokes.setup: Homer encouraging reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Calling the more successful Simon the untalented one
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of common perception about Simon and Garfunkel's talents

jokes.text: You mean you put nothing over nothing.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he chose family over career
jokes.punchline: Chong pointing out both are worthless
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty delivered with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: Don't make the mistake I always do. Of thinking too much before you act.
jokes.setup: Chong giving advice about spontaneity
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission that overthinking is his problem
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Chong recognizing his own tendency toward paralysis through analysis

jokes.text: I'm sorry I mocked your lack of suspenders. I guess I was just jealous of your belt.
jokes.setup: Comedy partners reconciling
jokes.punchline: Apology that creates new conflict about clothing choices
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where apology creates new petty jealousy

jokes.text: This belt has caused too many fights between us.
jokes.setup: Partners discussing clothing jealousy
jokes.punchline: Blaming inanimate object for relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Absurd personification of belt as relationship problem source

jokes.text: How now mad spirit, before we part, tis I Mischevious stand puckish, Bart. Was not I the players did disturb twas the doings of a green and naughty herb. The Gods have righted every wrong. Cheech the stoner, has his Chong.
jokes.setup: Bart delivering Shakespearean epilogue
jokes.punchline: Parodying Midsummer Night's Dream while wrapping up the episode
jokes.explanation: Clever Shakespeare parody tying to episode title but overly long for the payoff

jokes.text: Who is it? It's me, Dave, man. Open up. I got the stuff. Who? Dave, man. Dave's not here. Hold on, let me get the door here.
jokes.setup: Classic Dave's not here bit
jokes.punchline: Homer ruins it by actually opening the door
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal-mindedness ruins the classic bit - good character consistency

tmdb_overview: When Cheech and Chong stop in Springfield while on their reunion tour, Homer impresses Cheech so much he asks him to replace Chong; Principal Skinner forms a new comedy team with Chong; Marge tries to help a hoarder.
